description_long: This object consists of two distinct vases placed side by side, each with unique decorative features. The vase on the left is bulbous with a flared rim, ornate relief patterns resembling stylized leaves or petals, and a weathered, earthy coloration with hints of green patina. The vase on the right is more elongated and narrow, with a simpler, matte surface and subtle, shallow reliefs that appear to depict figures or abstract motifs. Both vases have small, rounded handles near their necks and wide openings at the top, suitable for holding flowers or other items. The surfaces show signs of age, with some discoloration and roughness, suggesting they are made from ceramic or stone. These vases would be fitting as decorative elements in a public park, garden, or urban plaza.
description: A pair of weathered, decorative vases with ornate reliefs and earthy tones, featuring bulbous and elongated forms. Both have wide openings and small handles, showing signs of age and use.
description_view_0: Front view showing both vases side by side, highlighting the ornate relief on the left vase and the simpler, matte surface of the right vase.
description_view_1: Rear view focusing on the back of the right vase, revealing its elongated shape and subtle reliefs, with the left vase partially obscured.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the full profile of the right vase and the left vase's decorative patterns, emphasizing their contrasting forms.
description_view_3: Angled view of the left vase, showcasing its bulbous body, flared rim, and detailed relief work.
